aerosol_index_335_367.title: UV Aerosol Index (335–367 nm)
aerosol_index_335_367.description: Absorbing Aerosol Index derived from UV radiances at 335 and 367 nm. Indicates the presence of UV-absorbing aerosols such as dust, smoke and volcanic ash.

aerosol_index_340_380.title: UV Aerosol Index (340–380 nm)
aerosol_index_340_380.description: Absorbing Aerosol Index calculated from radiances at 340 and 380 nm. Positive values indicate absorbing aerosols such as mineral dust, biomass burning smoke or volcanic ash.

aerosol_index_354_388.title: UV Aerosol Index (354–388 nm)
aerosol_index_354_388.description: Absorbing Aerosol Index derived from wavelengths 354 and 388 nm, commonly used to detect elevated aerosol layers such as desert dust and smoke plumes.

carbonmonoxide_total_column.title: Carbon Monoxide Total Column
carbonmonoxide_total_column.description: Total atmospheric column of carbon monoxide retrieved from Sentinel-5P TROPOMI measurements. CO is mainly produced by incomplete combustion processes and biomass burning.

nitrogendioxide_tropospheric_column.title: Tropospheric Nitrogen Dioxide Column
nitrogendioxide_tropospheric_column.description: Tropospheric vertical column density of nitrogen dioxide (NO₂). This gas is mainly emitted by fossil fuel combustion, traffic and industrial activities and is an important air quality indicator.

methane_mixing_ratio.title: Methane Mixing Ratio
methane_mixing_ratio.description: Column-averaged dry-air mixing ratio of methane (CH₄). Methane is a powerful greenhouse gas emitted by natural sources, agriculture, wetlands and fossil fuel production.

ozone_total_vertical_column.title: Total Ozone Column
ozone_total_vertical_column.description: Total vertical column of atmospheric ozone retrieved from Sentinel-5P measurements. Ozone in the stratosphere protects the Earth from harmful ultraviolet radiation.

sulfurdioxide_total_vertical_column.title: Sulfur Dioxide Total Column
sulfurdioxide_total_vertical_column.description: Total atmospheric column of sulfur dioxide (SO₂). Major sources include volcanic eruptions, industrial emissions and combustion of sulfur-containing fuels.

area_queimada.title: Área Queimada (MODIS)
area_queimada.description: Mapa mensal de área queimada derivado do sensor MODIS. Valores indicam ocorrência de áreas afetadas por queimadas.

cloud_optical_thickness.title: Cloud Optical Thickness
cloud_optical_thickness.description: Cloud optical thickness indicates how much light is attenuated by clouds. Higher values represent thicker clouds.

cloud_fraction.title: Cloud Fraction
cloud_fraction.description: Fraction of cloud cover within the satellite pixel. Values range from 0 (clear sky) to 1 (fully cloudy).

cloud_top_height.title: Cloud Top Height
cloud_top_height.description: Height of the cloud top above sea level.

cloud_base_height.title: Cloud Base Height
cloud_base_height.description: Height of the cloud base above sea level.

cloud_top_pressure.title: Cloud Top Pressure
cloud_top_pressure.description: Atmospheric pressure at the cloud top. Lower values correspond to higher altitude clouds.

cloud_base_pressure.title: Cloud Base Pressure
cloud_base_pressure.description: Atmospheric pressure at the cloud base.

formaldehyde_tropospheric_vertical_colum.title: Tropospheric Formaldehyde Column
formaldehyde_tropospheric_vertical_colum.description: Tropospheric vertical column density of formaldehyde (HCHO). Formaldehyde is an important intermediate product in the oxidation of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) and is widely used as an indicator of atmospheric pollution and biomass burning.
